verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 has no TM domain of its own but is covalently (disulfide via LAP-Cys33) anchored to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane through TM partners GARP (LRRC32) on activated FOXP3+ Tregs and platelets, and LRRC33 on macrophages/microglia. This wash-resistant covalent tethering to a TM scaffold meets the stable_surface_attachment criterion. Surface display is restricted to specific cell types under activation states, not a baseline feature. The dominant biology is secretion into the ECM (LLC/LTBP/fibrillin axis), but the documented cell-surface pool via GARP/LRRC33 disulfide anchoring qualifies as contextual.
